mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust corporate hiring, which correlates with higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases, the hypothesis is invalidated.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) reflects stronger aggregate demand, which typically boosts corporate revenues and stock valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 30% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPI (CPIAUCSL) and rising FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures and monetary tightening, creating uncertainty that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combined signal occurs but SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 7% signals tighter labor markets, which historically correlate with higher equity valuations due to improved corporate pricing power and consumer confidence.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 7% but 5-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 4%) creates a 'stagflation' risk, which typically depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower earnings growth prospects.
failure_condition: If the combined signal occurs but SPX returns are positive in > 50% of cases, the hypothesis is invalidated.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 40% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates stronger demand for residential construction, which correlates with higher consumer confidence and economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases but 60-day SPX returns are negative in > 30% of cases, the hypothesis fails.
